channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Americas Program Internship (Summer 2026) at the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)—a leading global policy think tank based in Washington, D.C., United States. This is a competitive, paid internship designed for individuals interested in international affairs, policy research, and regional dynamics across Latin America and the Caribbean. […]

The post CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Americas Program Internship (Summer 2026) at the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)—a leading global policy think tank based in Washington, D.C., United States.

This is a competitive, paid internship designed for individuals interested in international affairs, policy research, and regional dynamics across Latin America and the Caribbean.

About the Internship

The CSIS Americas Program focuses on key issues shaping the Western Hemisphere, including:

Democratic resilience
Citizen security
Economic competitiveness
Regional integration
Energy transitions
Global influence of Latin America and the Caribbean

As an intern, you will work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment supporting research, events, and policy engagement.

Internship Details

Duration: May 26 – September 10, 2026
Hours: Full-time (35 hours per week)
Location: Washington, D.C. (in-person)

Compensation

Interns are paid hourly based on education level:

$18/hour – Undergraduate students
$19/hour – Bachelor’s degree holders
$20/hour – Master’s degree holders

What You’ll Do

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting research and policy projects
Compiling literature reviews, summaries, and reports
Assisting with event planning and execution
Editing publications and drafting communications
Monitoring regional developments in the Western Hemisphere
Supporting outreach and communications activities

What They’re Looking For

Strong candidates will demonstrate:

A strong academic background in international affairs or related fields
Excellent research, analytical, and writing skills
Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks
Interest in Latin America and the Caribbean
Fluency in Spanish (required); Portuguese is a plus
Strong communication and organizational skills

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should have:

A bachelor’s degree (or be currently pursuing one) in a relevant field
Experience in research, writing, or office environments
Authorization to work in the United States

Why You Should Apply

This internship offers hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading policy institutions, providing exposure to real-world research, policymaking, and international affairs.

It is an excellent opportunity for anyone looking to build a career in public policy, global development, or international relations.

Application Deadline

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, but must be submitted by April 17, 2026.

How to Apply

Submit your application (CV + cover letter) via the official portal.

For more internship roles, Visit OFY here.

The post CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fund Our Freedoms “Build – Earn – Thrive” Initiative 2026: Up to $50,000 for Entrepreneurship Projects Supporting LGBTIQ+ Economic Inclusion
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Fund Our Freedoms – Build, Earn, Thrive (BET) Initiative 2026, a grant opportunity designed to support entrepreneurship-driven projects that advance economic inclusion for marginalized communities. This initiative is led by Outright International, focusing on empowering LGBTIQ+ individuals to access sustainable livelihoods and build long-term economic independence. About the Initiative […]

The post Fund Our Freedoms “Build – Earn – Thrive” Initiative 2026: Up to $50,000 for Entrepreneurship Projects Supporting LGBTIQ+ Economic Inclusion appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Fund Our Freedoms – Build, Earn, Thrive (BET) Initiative 2026, a grant opportunity designed to support entrepreneurship-driven projects that advance economic inclusion for marginalized communities.

This initiative is led by Outright International, focusing on empowering LGBTIQ+ individuals to access sustainable livelihoods and build long-term economic independence.

About the Initiative

The Build – Earn – Thrive (BET) Initiative aims to create inclusive economic systems where individuals—regardless of identity—have equal opportunities to participate in and benefit from economic activity.

The programme supports projects that help LGBTIQ+ individuals overcome barriers to employment and entrepreneurship, especially in regions where discrimination limits access to opportunities.

Funding Details

Maximum Grant: $50,000 USD
Project Duration: 18–24 months
Anticipated Start Date: July 1, 2026

This funding supports initiatives that demonstrate sustainability, scalability, and long-term impact.

Focus Area

The initiative focuses on entrepreneurship development, including:

Business training and mentorship
Business plan development and market analysis
Business incubation and startup support
Building access to formal labor markets
Strengthening financial inclusion and income generation

Projects that combine entrepreneurship with advocacy, education, or systemic change efforts are highly encouraged.

What They’re Looking For

Strong proposals should:

Demonstrate clear experience and capacity
Include a solid market analysis
Present a scalable and sustainable business model
Show measurable outcomes such as income growth, financial inclusion, and market access
Outline a plan for growth beyond the grant period

Ineligible Activities

The fund does not support:

Direct cash transfers or seed funding
Purchase of inventory for resale

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, organisations must:

Be operating in eligible countries
Have the capacity to implement entrepreneurship-focused programmes
Demonstrate experience working with marginalized communities

Eligible Countries

Kenya
Mozambique
Namibia
South Africa
Zambia

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by May 4, 2026 (11:59 PM ET).

How to Apply

Submit your application via Foundant.

Access Code: Showtime!

For technical support, contact: grants@outrightinternational.org

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Fund Our Freedoms “Build – Earn – Thrive” Initiative 2026: Up to $50,000 for Entrepreneurship Projects Supporting LGBTIQ+ Economic Inclusion appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Unlocking Scientific Solutions for Africa: The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant 2026
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant (JWO Research Grant) is a prestigious research funding opportunity designed to empower early-career scientists whose work addresses critical scientific and environmental challenges in Africa. Established to honor the legacy of the late Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer and her passion for science, the environment, and African development, this grant supports innovative […]

The post Unlocking Scientific Solutions for Africa: The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant (JWO Research Grant) is a prestigious research funding opportunity designed to empower early-career scientists whose work addresses critical scientific and environmental challenges in Africa. Established to honor the legacy of the late Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer and her passion for science, the environment, and African development, this grant supports innovative research with meaningful impact across the continent.

With a strong focus on driving evidence-based solutions, the JWO Research Grant invites applications from researchers who are committed to tackling pressing issues—from biodiversity conservation to climate resilience and sustainable development. This grant not only provides financial support but also fosters a collaborative research community, offering recipients a platform to share insights and scale their work.

Why the JWO Research Grant Matters

Scientific research is essential for addressing complex problems that affect societies and ecosystems. In Africa, where diverse environmental and social challenges intersect, research plays a pivotal role in informing policy, guiding sustainable practices, and enhancing community well-being. The JWO Research Grant recognizes this need and seeks to catalyze impactful research by supporting early-career scientists with visionary ideas.

The grant is especially significant for researchers who may face barriers in accessing funding. By offering substantial financial backing and recognition, the JWO Research Grant helps bridge the gap between innovative research proposals and real-world implementation. Previous recipients have conducted transformative work in areas such as soil biodiversity, ecosystem management, and climate-adaptive solutions.

Application Details and Deadline

The application window for the 2026 JWO Research Grant opened on 23 March 2026 and closes on 1 May 2026. Full proposals for invited candidates will be due by 3 July 2026, with interviews for the top finalists scheduled for August 17–21, 2026. The successful recipient will be announced at the annual Oppenheimer Research Conference in October 2026.

Grant Benefits

Selected recipients of the JWO Research Grant receive a financial award of USD $150,000, which can be used to advance their research goals. Beyond funding, recipients gain visibility within the international research community and access to networks that can support future collaborations and professional growth. Previous winners have leveraged this platform to expand their research scope and contribute to policy discussions in their fields.

What the Grant Supports

The JWO Research Grant is open to proposals that address scientific questions with clear relevance to African challenges. Past projects have focused on topics such as:

  • Strengthening freshwater ecosystems for climate resilience
  • Soil biodiversity conservation for climate-smart agriculture
  • Marine conservation and community engagement
  • Space science applications for disaster management
  • Plant-pollinator network resilience and food security
  • Coastal ecosystem restoration for livelihoods and biodiversity

These diverse themes reflect the grant’s commitment to supporting interdisciplinary and high-impact research that aligns with Africa’s development priorities.

Who Should Apply

Early-career scientists with doctoral degrees or equivalent research experience are encouraged to apply. Successful applicants typically demonstrate strong research proposals with clear objectives, feasible methodologies, and potential for significant impact. Proposals that emphasize sustainability, innovation, and community relevance are especially competitive.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can begin the application process via the official grant website. Applicants are advised to review all eligibility criteria and submission requirements carefully. Preparing a compelling proposal that clearly articulates the research problem, approach, expected outcomes, and broader significance will enhance the chances of success.

The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant offers a remarkable opportunity for early-career researchers to secure funding, amplify their work, and contribute to scientific progress in Africa. With its substantial financial support and commitment to impactful research, the JWO Research Grant is a catalyst for innovation and sustainable solutions. Researchers passionate about advancing science for Africa’s future are encouraged to apply before the 1 May 2026 deadline.

 

Deadline : 1 May 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this grant , click here.

For more grant opportunities, click here.

The post Unlocking Scientific Solutions for Africa: The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Responsible AI
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded international programme designed to empower the next generation of leaders in artificial intelligence governance. Organized by Globethics , this fellowship targets early-career professionals who are passionate about ensuring that AI development is ethical, inclusive, and aligned with global human […]

The post Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Responsible AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded international programme designed to empower the next generation of leaders in artificial intelligence governance. Organized by Globethics , this fellowship targets early-career professionals who are passionate about ensuring that AI development is ethical, inclusive, and aligned with global human values.

As artificial intelligence continues to transform industries and societies, the need for ethical oversight and governance has become more urgent than ever. This fellowship equips participants with the tools, networks, and real-world experience needed to influence policy, innovation, and implementation in AI systems worldwide.

About the Fellowship

The Globethics fellowship is uniquely structured to bring together professionals from diverse sectors, including academia, government, civil society, and the private sector. It focuses on values-driven AI governance, emphasizing human rights, inclusivity, accountability, and sustainability.

Participants will engage in interdisciplinary learning, collaborate with global experts, and contribute to impactful projects that address pressing challenges in AI governance. The programme fosters a global perspective, enabling fellows to understand how ethical AI frameworks can be applied across different cultural and socio-economic contexts.

Key Features of the Fellowship

One of the standout aspects of this fellowship is its comprehensive approach, combining academic learning, practical application, and international exposure.

Phase I: Core Programme

Selected fellows will participate in a robust foundational programme that includes:

  • Geneva Residency (6–10 July 2026):
    Fellows will attend an in-person residency in Geneva, a global hub for diplomacy and international governance. This experience offers direct insight into global policy-making processes.
  • Participation in the AI for Good Global Summit:
    Fellows will engage with world leaders, innovators, and policymakers shaping the future of AI.
  • Expert Mentorship:
    Participants will receive guidance from leading experts in AI ethics and governance.
  • Fully Funded Training:
    Accommodation and programme costs during the residency are covered, removing financial barriers to participation.
  • Project Development:
    Fellows will conceptualize and develop a research, policy, or innovation project addressing real-world AI governance challenges.
  • Global Networking Opportunities:
    Participants gain access to an international network of professionals and alumni, with opportunities for collaboration, publication, and visibility.

Phase II: Advanced Programme (Competitive Selection)

Top-performing fellows from Phase I will advance to a more intensive second phase, which includes:

  • Seed funding to support project implementation
  • Advanced mentorship and technical support
  • Opportunity to present at the Global Ethics Forum (October 2026)
  • Increased exposure to global stakeholders, policymakers, and funders

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is open to early-career professionals aged 24–35 who demonstrate a strong interest or involvement in AI ethics and governance.

Eligible applicants may come from:

  • Academia (Master’s, PhD, or postdoctoral researchers)
  • Government and public institutions
  • Private sector (technology, AI, compliance, innovation)
  • Civil society and interfaith organizations

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Active engagement in AI ethics, governance, or responsible innovation
  • Ability to design a research or policy-driven project
  • Interest in intercultural or interfaith dialogue
  • Strong motivation to contribute to ethical AI development

Notably, the programme is inclusive and does not require any religious affiliation.

Application Process

To apply for the fellowship, candidates must submit a single PDF document containing:

  • A CV (maximum of 2 pages)
  • A 500-word statement of interest detailing:
    • Relevant background and experience
    • Motivation for applying
    • Proposed project idea
  • Optional supporting materials such as publications, portfolio, or recommendation letters

Applications are submitted online through the official application platform.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: 26 March 2026
  • Selection Results: By 24 April 2026
  • Welcome Webinar: June 2026
  • Geneva Residency: 6–10 July 2026
  • Project Development Phase: July – September 2026

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship goes beyond traditional academic programmes by offering practical, real-world impact opportunities. Participants are not only trained but also empowered to implement solutions that address global AI governance challenges.

Key benefits include:

  • Building a strong international network in AI governance
  • Gaining exposure on global platforms
  • Developing impactful projects with real-world applications
  • Enhancing leadership, research, and policy-making skills

The opportunity to engage in Geneva further enhances the experience, placing fellows at the center of global governance discussions.

About Globethics

Globethics is an international non-governmental organization with consultative status at the United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC). The organization is dedicated to promoting ethical leadership, responsible governance, and sustainable development.

Through its initiatives, Globethics plays a critical role in advancing global conversations around ethical AI, human rights, and social justice.

Application Deadline: 16 April 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity,  click here

For more fellowship opportunities, click here.

The post Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Responsible AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Country Manager Germany (Remote, Munich Preferred) | Lead Impact-Driven Change in Global Education
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Are you a strategic leader passionate about transforming lives through education? Street Child Germany e.V. is offering a unique opportunity to step into a high-impact leadership role as Country Manager Germany. This position places you at the forefront of a mission to ensure every child has access to safe and quality education.With operations rooted in […]

The post Country Manager Germany (Remote, Munich Preferred) | Lead Impact-Driven Change in Global Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a strategic leader passionate about transforming lives through education? Street Child Germany e.V. is offering a unique opportunity to step into a high-impact leadership role as Country Manager Germany. This position places you at the forefront of a mission to ensure every child has access to safe and quality education.With operations rooted in Germany and impact spanning vulnerable communities worldwide, this role combines leadership, fundraising, and operational excellence in one dynamic position.

About Street Child Germany

Street Child is a globally recognized organization dedicated to improving access to education in some of the world’s toughest environments. Its German arm, Street Child Germany e.V., plays a critical role in mobilizing resources and partnerships to support this mission.The German team is small but highly mission-driven, working collaboratively to deliver measurable impact.

Role Overview: Country Manager Germany

Location: Remote within Germany (Munich area preferred)

Start Date: 01 May 2026

Workload: 80–100%

As Country Manager, you will lead the organization’s strategy, fundraising, operations, and growth in Germany. Reporting directly to the Board, you will collaborate with international partners to scale impact and drive sustainable funding.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership & Growth

  • Represent and position the organization as a trusted non-profit in Germany
  • Drive visibility, partnerships, and growth opportunities
  • Collaborate with the Board on strategy, governance, and risk

Fundraising & Partnerships

  • Develop and execute Germany’s fundraising strategy
  • Lead grant applications and donor reporting
  • Build relationships with foundations, corporates, and philanthropists

Operations & Team Management

  • Oversee finance, HR, administration, and compliance
  • Manage and support the fundraising team
  • Coordinate with the international network

Candidate Profile

Essential Requirements

  • Must reside in Germany (travel to Munich required if remote)
  • Strong alignment with the organization’s mission
  • Native-level German and strong English proficiency
  • Proven leadership and stakeholder management skills
  • Ability to work independently in a small team environment

Highly Desirable

  • Experience in German fundraising (foundations, corporates, philanthropists)
  • Knowledge of institutional donors (BMZ, EU, Auswärtiges Amt)
  • Familiarity with German non-profit law and governance

What You’ll Gain

  • A meaningful leadership role in a fast-growing global organization
  • Opportunity to shape the future of Street Child Germany
  • Access to an experienced board and international network
  • Flexible working structure with a Munich-based office

Diversity & Safeguarding

Street Child Germany is committed to diversity and inclusion. Applications are welcome from all qualified individuals regardless of background.

All offers are subject to reference checks, criminal record checks, and safeguarding compliance.

Application Process

Submit your CV and a short motivation letter (in English) via the official application link.

Deadline: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis (apply as early as possible).

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here .

For more job opportunities, click here.

The post Country Manager Germany (Remote, Munich Preferred) | Lead Impact-Driven Change in Global Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Center for Development Research (ZEF) at the University of Bonn is inviting applications for the prestigious BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026. This fully funded doctoral opportunity is designed for talented young researchers from the Global South who are passionate about sustainable development and interdisciplinary research. Through its structured doctoral program, the Bonn International Graduate School for […]

The post BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Center for Development Research (ZEF) at the University of Bonn is inviting applications for the prestigious BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026. This fully funded doctoral opportunity is designed for talented young researchers from the Global South who are passionate about sustainable development and interdisciplinary research.

Through its structured doctoral program, the Bonn International Graduate School for Development Research (BIGS-DR), ZEF provides an exceptional academic environment for scholars aiming to address some of the world’s most pressing global challenges.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the BIGS-DR Scholarships is August 31, 2026.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their research proposals and supporting documents well in advance to ensure a competitive application.

About the Programme

The BIGS-DR doctoral programme is an interdisciplinary PhD track that combines academic excellence with practical relevance. It supports students throughout their doctoral journey, from initial coursework to final dissertation.

Participants benefit from:

  • Structured doctoral training modules
  • Interdisciplinary research approaches
  • Soft skills and career development training
  • Close supervision by international experts

ZEF hosts approximately 50 senior researchers from diverse disciplines, providing a rich intellectual environment where doctoral candidates can thrive and develop innovative research solutions.

Research Focus Areas

Applicants are expected to submit a creative and impactful research proposal aligned with sustainable development themes. Relevant topics include:

  • Sustainable food systems
  • Agriculture and rural development
  • Nutrition and public health
  • Climate change and environmental sustainability
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Migration and development
  • Governance and institutional frameworks
  • Conflict and development dynamics

The programme strongly encourages interdisciplinary approaches that connect multiple fields to address complex global challenges.

Funding and Scholarships

The BIGS-DR programme offers EPOS scholarships, funded by the DAAD (Deutscher Akademischer Austauschdienst).

These scholarships typically cover:

  • Monthly stipend
  • Travel costs
  • Health insurance
  • Academic and research support

The funding aims to enable scholars to focus fully on their research while gaining international academic exposure.

Academic Environment and Benefits

ZEF is recognized as a leading global institute for development research. As part of the programme, doctoral candidates will:

  • Join a diverse, international cohort of researchers
  • Engage in collaborative and interdisciplinary learning
  • Access world-class academic supervision
  • Participate in workshops, seminars, and global research networks

This environment fosters both academic excellence and professional growth, preparing scholars for careers in academia, international organizations, policy-making, and development practice.

Why Choose BIGS-DR at ZEF

The BIGS-DR programme stands out for its strong integration of research, policy, and practice. It equips scholars with the tools needed to generate impactful knowledge and contribute to sustainable development solutions worldwide.

By studying at one of Germany’s top universities and engaging with a global network of researchers, participants gain both academic credibility and practical expertise.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities click here 

The post BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme (FLP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique paid summer opportunity for current and recent undergraduate students passionate about climate and sustainability. This prestigious programme provides hands-on experience through real-world climate projects, professional development training, and networking opportunities with experts across academia, policy, and industry. Application Deadline […]

The post Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme (FLP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique paid summer opportunity for current and recent undergraduate students passionate about climate and sustainability.

This prestigious programme provides hands-on experience through real-world climate projects, professional development training, and networking opportunities with experts across academia, policy, and industry.

Application Deadline and Programme Dates

Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time).

The programme will run from:

  • Start Date: Monday, July 13, 2026
  • End Date: Friday, September 11, 2026

Participants are entitled to 5 days of annual leave during this period.

Programme Structure and Work Experience

Participants, known as Project Assistants, will be assigned to specific climate and sustainability projects hosted by Cambridge Zero and its partners.

Projects span a wide range of areas, including:

  • Climate policy and adaptation
  • Sustainability research
  • Climate education
  • Innovation and mapping initiatives
  • Environmental health and future housing

Project Assistants will:

  • Work collaboratively in professional environments
  • Engage with researchers, policymakers, and stakeholders
  • Contribute to meaningful outputs such as policy briefings, research reports, and communication materials

For example, in the Climate Adaptation Policy project, participants will:

  • Develop a policy briefing based on national climate reports
  • Conduct research and stakeholder consultations
  • Potentially contribute to government evidence submissions

Professional Development and Career Growth

Beyond project work, the Future Leaders Programme includes a structured development component designed to support participants’ long-term careers.

Participants will:

  • Explore diverse career pathways in climate and sustainability
  • Reflect on personal strengths and professional goals
  • Receive coaching and mentorship from experts
  • Build a strong peer network within their cohort

This combination of practical experience and personal development ensures participants gain valuable skills applicable to future academic pursuits and job opportunities.

Key Benefits of the Programme

  • Paid summer work experience in climate and sustainability
  • Opportunity to work on real-world, impactful projects
  • Direct engagement with professionals and experts
  • Structured career development and coaching
  • Access to a network of like-minded peers and alumni

Many past participants have highlighted the programme’s role in shaping their careers, helping them build both technical expertise and professional confidence.

Salary, Location, and Working Conditions

  • Salary: £13.45 per hour (aligned with the UK real Living Wage)
  • Working Hours: 37 hours per week
  • Location: In-person in Cambridge, United Kingdom
  • Flexibility: Some flexible working arrangements may be considered

Participants must be able to work in Cambridge for the duration of the programme.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to:

  • Current undergraduate students
  • Recent graduates

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Interest in climate and sustainability
  • Strong motivation to learn and contribute
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a professional setting

Why You Should Apply

The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme stands out as a comprehensive early-career opportunity in the climate field. It not only provides paid work experience but also equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to succeed in sustainability careers.

By combining hands-on project work with structured professional development, the programme prepares the next generation of climate leaders to tackle global environmental challenges effectively.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Graduate Application International Network (GAIN) Mentor Program 2026/2027 is a unique global volunteer opportunity designed for experienced academics and professionals who are passionate about increasing African representation in graduate education. This impactful mentorship initiative connects mentors with aspiring African students, guiding them through the complex graduate application process in fields such as economics, public […]

The post GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Graduate Application International Network (GAIN) Mentor Program 2026/2027 is a unique global volunteer opportunity designed for experienced academics and professionals who are passionate about increasing African representation in graduate education. This impactful mentorship initiative connects mentors with aspiring African students, guiding them through the complex graduate application process in fields such as economics, public policy, political science, and public health.

By becoming a GAIN mentor, you will play a direct role in shaping the academic and professional futures of talented African students while contributing to a broader mission of equity and inclusion in global academia.

About the GAIN Program

The GAIN program is a structured, multi-phase initiative that supports African students throughout their graduate application journey. It combines educational webinars, personalized mentorship, and financial support to reduce barriers to higher education.

The program is divided into three main components:

  1. Webinar Program
  2. Mentoring Program
  3. Alumni Support Network

Each component is designed to progressively equip participants with the knowledge, resources, and confidence needed to successfully apply to graduate schools worldwide.

The Role of a GAIN Mentor

GAIN mentors are at the heart of the program. They provide personalized guidance and support to mentees navigating the graduate application process.

Core Responsibilities

Mentors typically:

  • Guide one mentee through the entire application cycle
  • Conduct regular virtual meetings (usually bi-weekly)
  • Provide feedback on application materials such as CVs and statements of purpose
  • Offer advice on selecting universities and programs
  • Support preparation for standardized tests such as GRE and TOEFL
  • Share personal experiences and insights from their academic journeys

In addition to one-on-one mentoring, some mentors may choose to:

  • Support group sessions or workshops
  • Offer expertise in specific areas such as scholarship applications or essay editing
  • Participate in collaborative mentor networks or peer-support groups

Time Commitment and Duration

Mentors are expected to commit a moderate but consistent amount of time throughout the program.

Key Details

  • Commitment: Approximately 4–8 hours per month
  • Duration: May/June 2026 – April 2027
  • Format: Fully virtual engagement

This flexible structure allows mentors to balance their professional responsibilities while making a meaningful impact.

Who Typically Becomes a GAIN Mentor

GAIN mentors are usually individuals who have successfully navigated graduate education themselves and are willing to support others on the same path.

Typical Mentor Profiles

  • PhD students
  • Academic researchers
  • University professors
  • Policy professionals and advisors

Most mentors have firsthand experience with graduate applications and understand the challenges involved.

Eligibility Requirements for Mentors

To register as a GAIN mentor, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Required Criteria

  • Ability to commit 4–8 hours per month throughout the mentoring cycle
  • Experience completing a graduate application process in:
    • Economics
    • Public policy
    • Political science
    • Public health
    • Or related disciplines
  • Strong interest in increasing African representation in academia
  • Willingness to guide and support mentees consistently

Preferred Criteria

  • Previous mentoring experience
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and academic equity

Overview of the GAIN Program Structure

Understanding the full GAIN program helps mentors better support their mentees.

1. Webinar Program (January – June)

This foundational phase provides participants with essential knowledge about the graduate application process.

Topics covered include:

  • Scholarships and funding opportunities
  • GRE, TOEFL, and standardized test strategies
  • Writing academic CVs and statements of purpose
  • Securing letters of recommendation
  • Choosing universities and career paths
  • Balancing academic pursuits with personal responsibilities

Webinars are held every two weeks and feature leading African scholars and experts.

2. Mentoring Program

This is the core phase where mentors actively engage with mentees.

Key features include:

  • One-on-one mentor-mentee matching based on:
    • Field of study
    • Professional background
    • Country of origin
  • Bi-weekly meetings and continuous support
  • Guidance on application preparation and submission

The mentoring phase begins with a GRE summer bootcamp, providing participants with access to preparation materials and structured support.

3. Financial Support for Mentees

GAIN provides financial assistance to selected participants to reduce barriers to application.

Support includes:

  • Application fee coverage
  • Standardized test fees (GRE, TOEFL)
  • Score submission costs

High-performing mentees may qualify for Mentoring+, which offers:

  • Increased financial support
  • Enhanced mentorship engagement

4. GAIN Alumni Network

After successful admission into graduate programs, mentees transition into the GAIN Alumni Network.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly check-in sessions
  • Continued mentorship and guidance
  • Targeted webinars and academic support
  • Community building and networking opportunities

This ensures long-term success beyond admission.

Why You Should Become a GAIN Mentor

Becoming a GAIN mentor is not only an opportunity to give back but also a chance to grow professionally and personally.

Key Benefits

  • Contribute to increasing African representation in global academia
  • Support talented students in achieving their academic goals
  • Build meaningful professional relationships
  • Gain mentorship and leadership experience
  • Join a global network of academics and professionals

Application Process

To become a GAIN mentor:

  • Complete the Online Mentor Registration Form
  • Provide details about your academic and professional background
  • Indicate your availability and areas of expertise

Application Deadline

The deadline to register as a GAIN Mentor for the 2026/2027 cycle is:

April 30, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure placement in the program.

Click HERE to learn more about the GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027.

Discover more global Mentorship and African opportunities on OFY.

The post GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Institute for Human Rights and Business is currently seeking 1–2 experienced Researchers/Writers specializing in climate adaptation and resilience to lead a new phase of its flagship JUST Stories project. This is a unique global opportunity for professionals who combine rigorous research expertise with exceptional storytelling skills and a deep understanding of climate justice. This […]

The post Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Human Rights and Business is currently seeking 1–2 experienced Researchers/Writers specializing in climate adaptation and resilience to lead a new phase of its flagship JUST Stories project. This is a unique global opportunity for professionals who combine rigorous research expertise with exceptional storytelling skills and a deep understanding of climate justice.

This role sits at the intersection of climate action, human rights, and global development, offering the chance to contribute to a transformative initiative aimed at reshaping how climate adaptation is understood, financed, and implemented worldwide.

Application Deadline and Timeline

Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time).

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to:

  • First-round interviews: April 23–24, 2026
  • Second-round interviews: Scheduled the following week

The expected start date is April or May 2026, with the contract running through September 2027.

About the JUST Stories Project

The JUST Stories initiative, launched in 2024, is a global effort to identify and document real-world examples of effective, people-centered climate action. While the first phase focused on decarbonization, this second phase shifts attention toward climate adaptation and resilience.

The project is grounded in a critical insight: climate solutions fail when they exclude the communities most affected. By contrast, successful adaptation emerges from inclusive, locally grounded, and co-designed approaches.

Over the next 18 months, the project will:

  • Identify and document community-led adaptation solutions worldwide
  • Highlight best practices and enabling conditions
  • Translate these stories into actionable insights for policymakers, investors, and practitioners

These outputs are not merely narratives but serve as evidence-based tools to inform decision-making across finance, policy, and corporate sectors.

Role Overview

The selected Researchers/Writers will play a central role in shaping this next phase by producing immersive, field-based stories that bridge the gap between local realities and global decision-making systems.

The role requires the ability to operate across two critical dimensions:

  • Ground-level engagement with communities and frontline actors
  • System-level understanding of finance, governance, and climate policy

Key Responsibilities

Research and Analysis

  • Conduct in-depth literature reviews on climate adaptation, resilience, and locally-led approaches
  • Identify high-impact case studies across diverse global contexts
  • Develop frameworks to assess effective and equitable adaptation

Fieldwork and Primary Research

  • Design and conduct interviews with community leaders, institutions, and workers
  • Undertake international field visits to gather firsthand data and narratives
  • Ensure ethical research practices, including consent and safeguarding

Narrative Development

  • Produce high-quality, publication-ready stories integrating human experience and analytical insight
  • Translate complex findings into clear, actionable recommendations
  • Collaborate with communications teams to create multi-format outputs (articles, briefings, audio, visual content)

Strategic Contribution

  • Support cross-sector engagement involving policymakers, investors, and civil society
  • Contribute to synthesizing lessons across case studies
  • Participate in global convenings such as Climate Weeks and COP events

Required Expertise and Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least 7 years of experience in research and writing
  • Proven expertise in climate adaptation and resilience
  • Strong background in qualitative research and fieldwork
  • Exceptional storytelling and analytical writing abilities
  • Understanding of global frameworks such as UN climate and human rights standards

Experience in working with Indigenous communities, NGOs, or climate finance actors is highly valued.

Contract Details and Compensation

  • Type: Consultancy contract under UK law
  • Duration: April/May 2026 – September 2027
  • Compensation: Approximately £70,000 total budget
  • Structure Options:
    • One full-time lead researcher/writer
    • Two part-time contributors

Candidates must:

  • Have the legal right to work in their country
  • Provide their own equipment and internet access
  • Be available for international travel (up to three times per year)

Work Environment

This is a fully remote role open globally. However, candidates must be located in time zones compatible with Central European Time (CET) or westward to ensure collaboration with teams operating on UK and West Africa time.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role represents more than a writing or research position. It is an opportunity to directly influence how climate adaptation is conceptualized and implemented globally.

By documenting and amplifying community-led solutions, the selected candidates will help shift dominant narratives away from top-down approaches toward inclusive, effective, and sustainable climate action.

Professionals passionate about climate justice, human rights, and impactful storytelling are strongly encouraged to apply.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here  

The post Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026 program is now officially open, offering a significant funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations working to strengthen local communities. With a total of $2.5 million in grants available, this initiative reflects NBCUniversal’s commitment to empowering grassroots organizations and fostering sustainable community development. This annual grant program is designed to support […]

The post NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026 program is now officially open, offering a significant funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations working to strengthen local communities. With a total of $2.5 million in grants available, this initiative reflects NBCUniversal’s commitment to empowering grassroots organizations and fostering sustainable community development.

This annual grant program is designed to support nonprofit organizations operating in select markets where NBC and Telemundo-owned television stations are present. By providing unrestricted funding, NBCUniversal ensures that recipient organizations have the flexibility to allocate resources where they are most needed, enabling them to maximize their impact.

Application Timeline and Deadline

Applications for the 2026 NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants will be accepted from March 24, 2026, through April 24, 2026.

All applications must be submitted before 7:59:59 PM Eastern Time on April 24, 2026. Late submissions will not be considered under any circumstances, making it essential for applicants to plan and submit their proposals well in advance of the deadline.

Funding Overview

NBCUniversal will award grants to eligible nonprofit organizations with annual operating expenses ranging between $100,000 and $1,000,000. The grants are categorized as general operating funds, meaning recipients are not restricted to specific project expenditures. Instead, funding can support broader organizational needs, including staffing, program expansion, infrastructure, and operational sustainability.

Key Focus Areas

The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants program prioritizes organizations that are actively working within three major thematic areas:

1. Youth Education and Empowerment

This category supports programs that equip young people with essential tools for success. Initiatives may include:

  • STEM and STEAM education programs
  • Youth entrepreneurship development
  • In-school and community-based educational support
  • Skill-building and leadership development initiatives

Organizations that focus on preparing youth for future academic and professional success are strongly encouraged to apply.

2. Next Generation Storytellers

This category is dedicated to nurturing emerging voices and creative talent. Eligible programs include:

  • Media and communications training
  • Arts, journalism, and storytelling initiatives
  • Opportunities in sports and entertainment industries
  • Platforms that amplify youth voices and perspectives

This focus area aims to diversify and strengthen the pipeline of talent in storytelling and media industries.

3. Community Engagement

Programs under this category aim to strengthen social cohesion and civic participation. Examples include:

  • Volunteerism initiatives
  • Community-building events and programs
  • Projects that encourage local engagement and collaboration
  • Efforts to foster unity and inclusivity within communities

Organizations working to create meaningful connections and engagement at the community level will find strong alignment with this category.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants, applicants must:

  • Be a registered nonprofit organization
  • Operate within one of the eligible NBC or Telemundo station markets
  • Have total annual expenses between $100,000 and $1,000,000
  • Demonstrate measurable impact within their local community
  • Align their work with at least one of the three focus areas

Application Process

Interested organizations must complete their applications through the official NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants portal. Additionally, applicants are encouraged to register for informational webinars hosted by NBCUniversal to gain insights into the application process, evaluation criteria, and best practices for submission.

Applicants should ensure that their proposals clearly articulate their mission, impact, and alignment with NBCUniversal’s priority areas. Strong applications typically highlight measurable outcomes, community relevance, and sustainability.

Why This Grant Matters

The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants stand out due to their flexibility and emphasis on local impact. By offering unrestricted funding, the program recognizes the importance of empowering nonprofits to address community needs in dynamic and adaptive ways.

Moreover, the focus on youth, storytelling, and community engagement reflects a forward-looking approach to social development—one that values innovation, inclusivity, and grassroots leadership.

Organizations seeking to expand their reach, strengthen their operations, or deepen their community impact should strongly consider applying for this opportunity.

For more information click here

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
